heading: Relief from joint and several liability on joint return
label: 6015 Relief from joint and several liability on joint return

Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “The individual may petition the Tax Court (and the Tax Court shall have jurisdiction) to determine the appropriate relief available to the individual under this section if such petition is filed during the 90-day period beginning on the date on which the Secretary mails by certified or registered mail a notice to such individual of the Secretary’s determination of relief available to the individual. children.text: an individual who has made a joint return may elect to seek relief under the procedures prescribed under subsection (b); and

if such individual is eligible to elect the application of subsection (c), such individual may, in addition to any election under paragraph (1), elect to limit such individual’s liability for any deficiency with respect to such joint return in the manner prescribed under subsection (c).

children.heading: Procedures for relief from liability applicable to all joint filers
children.label: b Procedures for relief from liability applicable to all joint filers
A
children.text: a joint return has been made for a taxable year;

children.label: B
children.text: on such return there is an understatement of tax attributable to erroneous items of one individual filing the joint return;

children.label: C
children.text: the other individual filing the joint return establishes that in signing the return he or she did not know, and had no reason to know, that there was such understatement;

children.label: D
children.text: taking into account all the facts and circumstances, it is inequitable to hold the other individual liable for the deficiency in tax for such taxable year attributable to such understatement; and

children.label: E
children.text: the other individual elects (in such form as the Secretary may prescribe) the benefits of this subsection not later than the date which is 2 years after the date the Secretary has begun collection activities with respect to the individual making the election,

children.heading: Apportionment of relief
children.label: 2 Apportionment of relief
children.text: If an individual who, but for paragraph (1)(C), would be relieved of liability under paragraph (1), establishes that in signing the return such individual did not know, and had no reason to know, the extent of such understatement, then such individual shall be relieved of liability for tax (including interest, penalties, and other amounts) for such taxable year to the extent that such liability is attributable to the portion of such understatement of which such individual did not know and had no reason to know.

children.heading: Understatement
children.label: 3 Understatement
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, the term “understatement” has the meaning given to such term by section 6662(d)(2)(A).

children.heading: Procedures to limit liability for taxpayers no longer married or taxpayers legally separated or not living together
children.label: c Procedures to limit liability for taxpayers no longer married or taxpayers legally separated or not living together

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Except as provided in this subsection, if an individual who has made a joint return for any taxable year elects the application of this subsection, the individual’s liability for any deficiency which is assessed with respect to the return shall not exceed the portion of such deficiency properly allocable to the individual under subsection (d).

children.heading: Burden of proof
children.label: 2 Burden of proof
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraph (A)(ii) or (C) of paragraph (3), each individual who elects the application of this subsection shall have the burden of proof with respect to establishing the portion of any deficiency allocable to such individual.

children.heading: Individuals eligible to make election
children.label: A Individuals eligible to make election
I
children.text: at the time such election is filed, such individual is no longer married to, or is legally separated from, the individual with whom such individual filed the joint return to which the election relates; or

children.label: II
children.text: such individual was not a member of the same household as the individual with whom such joint return was filed at any time during the 12-month period ending on the date such election is filed.

children.heading: Certain taxpayers ineligible to elect
children.label: ii Certain taxpayers ineligible to elect
children.text: If the Secretary demonstrates that assets were transferred between individuals filing a joint return as part of a fraudulent scheme by such individuals, an election under this subsection by either individual shall be invalid (and section 6013(d)(3) shall apply to the joint return).

children.heading: Time for election
children.label: B Time for election
children.text: An election under this subsection for any taxable year may be made at any time after a deficiency for such year is asserted but not later than 2 years after the date on which the Secretary has begun collection activities with respect to the individual making the election.

children.heading: Election not valid with respect to certain deficiencies
children.label: C Election not valid with respect to certain deficiencies
children.text: If the Secretary demonstrates that an individual making an election under this subsection had actual knowledge, at the time such individual signed the return, of any item giving rise to a deficiency (or portion thereof) which is not allocable to such individual under subsection (d), such election shall not apply to such deficiency (or portion). This subparagraph shall not apply where the individual with actual knowledge establishes that such individual signed the return under duress.

children.heading: Liability increased by reason of transfers of property to avoid tax
children.label: 4 Liability increased by reason of transfers of property to avoid tax

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of this subsection, the portion of the deficiency for which the individual electing the application of this subsection is liable (without regard to this paragraph) shall be increased by the value of any disqualified asset transferred to the individual.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The term “disqualified asset” means any property or right to property transferred to an individual making the election under this subsection with respect to a joint return by the other individual filing such joint return if the principal purpose of the transfer was the avoidance of tax or payment of tax.

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: For purposes of clause (i), except as provided in subclause (II), any transfer which is made after the date which is 1 year before the date on which the first letter of proposed deficiency which allows the taxpayer an opportunity for administrative review in the Internal Revenue Service Independent Office of Appeals is sent shall be presumed to have as its principal purpose the avoidance of tax or payment of tax.

children.heading: Exceptions
children.label: II Exceptions
children.text: Subclause (I) shall not apply to any transfer pursuant to a decree of divorce or separate maintenance or a written instrument incident to such a decree or to any transfer which an individual establishes did not have as its principal purpose the avoidance of tax or payment of tax.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The portion of any deficiency on a joint return allocated to an individual shall be the amount which bears the same ratio to such deficiency as the net amount of items taken into account in computing the deficiency and allocable to the individual under paragraph (3) bears to the net amount of all items taken into account in computing the deficiency.

children.label: A
children.text: the disallowance of a credit; or

children.label: B
children.text: any tax (other than tax imposed by section 1 or 55) required to be included with the joint return;

children.heading: Allocation of items giving rise to the deficiency
children.label: 3 Allocation of items giving rise to the deficiency

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Except as provided in paragraphs (4) and (5), any item giving rise to a deficiency on a joint return shall be allocated to individuals filing the return in the same manner as it would have been allocated if the individuals had filed separate returns for the taxable year.

children.heading: Exception where other spouse benefits
children.label: B Exception where other spouse benefits
children.text: Under rules prescribed by the Secretary, an item otherwise allocable to an individual under subparagraph (A) shall be allocated to the other individual filing the joint return to the extent the item gave rise to a tax benefit on the joint return to the other individual.

children.heading: Exception for fraud
children.label: C Exception for fraud
children.text: The Secretary may provide for an allocation of any item in a manner not prescribed by subparagraph (A) if the Secretary establishes that such allocation is appropriate due to fraud of one or both individuals.

children.heading: Limitations on separate returns disregarded
children.label: 4 Limitations on separate returns disregarded
children.text: If an item of deduction or credit is disallowed in its entirety solely because a separate return is filed, such disallowance shall be disregarded and the item shall be computed as if a joint return had been filed and then allocated between the spouses appropriately. A similar rule shall apply for purposes of section 86.

children.heading: Child’s liability
children.label: 5 Child’s liability
children.text: If the liability of a child of a taxpayer is included on a joint return, such liability shall be disregarded in computing the separate liability of either spouse and such liability shall be allocated appropriately between the spouses.

children.heading: Petition for review by Tax Court
children.label: e Petition for review by Tax Court
I
children.text: the date the Secretary mails, by certified or registered mail to the taxpayer’s last known address, notice of the Secretary’s final determination of relief available to the individual, or

children.label: II
children.text: the date which is 6 months after the date such election is filed or request is made with the Secretary, and

children.label: ii
children.text: not later than the close of the 90th day after the date described in clause (i)(I).

children.heading: Restrictions applicable to collection of assessment
children.label: B Restrictions applicable to collection of assessment

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Except as otherwise provided in section 6851 or 6861, no levy or proceeding in court shall be made, begun, or prosecuted against the individual making an election under subsection (b) or (c) or requesting equitable relief under subsection (f) for collection of any assessment to which such election or request relates until the close of the 90th day referred to in subparagraph (A)(ii), or, if a petition has been filed with the Tax Court under subparagraph (A), until the decision of the Tax Court has become final. Rules similar to the rules of section 7485 shall apply with respect to the collection of such assessment.

children.heading: Authority to enjoin collection actions
children.label: ii Authority to enjoin collection actions
children.text: Notwithstanding the provisions of section 7421(a), the beginning of such levy or proceeding during the time the prohibition under clause (i) is in force may be enjoined by a proceeding in the proper court, including the Tax Court. The Tax Court shall have no jurisdiction under this subparagraph to enjoin any action or proceeding unless a timely petition has been filed under subparagraph (A) and then only in respect of the amount of the assessment to which the election under subsection (b) or (c) relates or to which the request under subsection (f) relates.

children.heading: Suspension of running of period of limitations
children.label: 2 Suspension of running of period of limitations
A
children.text: for the period during which the Secretary is prohibited by paragraph (1)(B) from collecting by levy or a proceeding in court and for 60 days thereafter, and

children.label: B
children.text: if a waiver under paragraph (5) is made, from the date the claim for relief was filed until 60 days after the waiver is filed with the Secretary.

children.label: A
children.text: the Tax Court shall lose jurisdiction of the individual’s action under this section to whatever extent jurisdiction is acquired by the district court or the United States Court of Federal Claims over the taxable years that are the subject of the suit for refund, and

children.label: B
children.text: the court acquiring jurisdiction shall have jurisdiction over the petition filed under this subsection.

children.heading: Notice to other spouse
children.label: 4 Notice to other spouse
children.text: The Tax Court shall establish rules which provide the individual filing a joint return but not making the election under subsection (b) or (c) or the request for equitable relief under subsection (f) with adequate notice and an opportunity to become a party to a proceeding under either such subsection.

children.heading: Waiver
children.label: 5 Waiver
children.text: An individual who elects the application of subsection (b) or (c) or who requests equitable relief under subsection (f) (and who agrees with the Secretary’s determination of relief) may waive in writing at any time the restrictions in paragraph (1)(B) with respect to collection of the outstanding assessment (whether or not a notice of the Secretary’s final determination of relief has been mailed).

children.heading: Suspension of running of period for filing petition in title 11 cases
children.label: 6 Suspension of running of period for filing petition in title 11 cases
children.text: In the case of a person who is prohibited by reason of a case under title 11, United States Code, from filing a petition under paragraph (1)(A) with respect to a final determination of relief under this section, the running of the period prescribed by such paragraph for filing such a petition with respect to such final determination shall be suspended for the period during which the person is so prohibited from filing such a petition, and for 60 days thereafter.

children.label: A
children.text: the administrative record established at the time of the determination, and

children.label: B
children.text: any additional newly discovered or previously unavailable evidence.

children.label: A
children.text: taking into account all the facts and circumstances, it is inequitable to hold the individual liable for any unpaid tax or any deficiency (or any portion of either), and

children.label: B
children.text: relief is not available to such individual under subsection (b) or (c),

children.label: A
children.text: has not been paid, provided that such request is made before the expiration of the applicable period of limitation under section 6502, or

children.label: B
children.text: has been paid, provided that such request is made during the period in which the individual could submit a timely claim for refund or credit of such payment.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Except as provided in paragraphs (2) and (3), notwithstanding any other law or rule of law (other than section 6511, 6512(b), 7121, or 7122), credit or refund shall be allowed or made to the extent attributable to the application of this section.

children.heading: Res judicata
children.label: 2 Res judicata
children.text: In the case of any election under subsection (b) or (c) or of any request for equitable relief under subsection (f), if a decision of a court in any prior proceeding for the same taxable year has become final, such decision shall be conclusive except with respect to the qualification of the individual for relief which was not an issue in such proceeding. The exception contained in the preceding sentence shall not apply if the court determines that the individual participated meaningfully in such prior proceeding.

children.heading: Credit and refund not allowed under subsection (c)
children.label: 3 Credit and refund not allowed under subsection (c)
children.text: No credit or refund shall be allowed as a result of an election under subsection (c).
regulations providing methods for allocation of items other than the methods under subsection (d)(3); and

regulations providing the opportunity for an individual to have notice of, and an opportunity to participate in, any administrative proceeding with respect to an election made under subsection (b) or (c) or a request for equitable relief made under subsection (f) by the other individual filing the joint return.
